channel.title: Business - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.description:

  2. Girl Scouts troop draws buzz after runaway cookie sales outside cannabis shop  Fox News
  3. Girl Scout troop's cookie booth outside Mount Laurel, New Jersey, marijuana dispensary draws praise, criticism  6abc Philadelphia
  4. Girl Scouts highly unimpressed after New Jersey troop teams up with local cannabis dispensary to sell cookies  The Independent
  5. Girl Scout troop sets up shop at weed dispensary. Cookies are in high demand.  NJ.com

item.title: Why are fewer Americans working the night shift? : The Indicator from Planet Money - NPR
item.description: Why are fewer Americans working the night shift? : The Indicator from Planet Money  NPR

item.title: Jack Dorsey Is Ready to Explain the Block Layoffs - WIRED
item.description:
1. Jack Dorsey Is Ready to Explain the Block Layoffs  WIRED
  2. Opinion | I Worked for Block. Its A.I. Job Cuts Aren’t What They Seem.  The New York Times
  3. The Week the Dreaded AI Jobs Wipeout Got Real  WSJ
  4. Block employee says the company dangled a 75% pay raise to get her to stay after layoffs — but she decided to quit  Business Insider
  5. Exclusive: Block’s CFO explains the AI leaps over 18 months that led to the decision to slash nearly half its workforce  Fortune

item.title: Iran Is Bombing Data Centers in Retaliation - Futurism
item.description:
1. Iran Is Bombing Data Centers in Retaliation  Futurism
  2. ‘It means missile defence on datacentres’: drone strikes raise doubts over Gulf as AI superpower  The Guardian
  3. Tech companies scramble to respond amid US-Israel war with Iran  BBC
  4. Amazon says drones hit 3 of its Middle East data centers amid Iran conflict  CBS News
  5. Banking, payments services disrupted after Amazon UAE data centers hit in drone strikes  CNBC
